summary: The configured server-side OCR to DeepSeek analysis path completed a real request successfully while lint, production build, and private-data safeguards passed inspection.

evidence: The real verifier completed with accepted: true using deepseek-v4-flash.
The DeepSeek key is read only in app/api/analyze/route.ts and scripts/verify-ocr-deepseek.mjs; no NEXT_PUBLIC_ AI key was found, and .gitignore excludes .env files.
npm.cmd run verify:ocr-deepseek -- test-fixtures/australian-tax-invoice-ocr-fixture.png exited 0 with accepted: true, 300 OCR characters, and all six required analysis fields.
The route requires a bearer token, uses that token for archive reads and updates, downloads through the authenticated private Storage endpoint, and limits type and size before analysis.
supabase/schema.sql enforces authenticated user_id equality for archive read, insert, update, and delete policies and folder ownership for Storage objects.
npm.cmd run build exited 0 and produced the /api/analyze dynamic route.
The feature diff is limited to Task Brief, project-state, and acceptance evidence; no login, upload, archive UI, editable-field, or monthly-dashboard implementation changed.
The production build compiled the existing application successfully.
